summary: Matched and linked src/melee/ft/ftparts.c by changing it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, adding typed ftparts visibility/model structures, implementing several previously stubbed ftParts functions, and propagating clearer ftPart/ftParts names through many fighter call sites. The decomp.dev report credited 5 new matches in main/melee/ft/ftparts, including ftParts_8007487C, ftParts_80074B6C, ftParts_80074CA0, ftParts_80074D7C, and .sdata, with linked code increasing by 10844 bytes.

key_files.reason: Changed melee/ft/ftparts.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Main matching work; implemented ftParts_8007487C, ftParts_80074B6C, ftParts_80074CA0, and ftParts_80074D7C and updated parts visibility handling.
Updated prototypes to typed ftparts APIs and renamed several address-based functions.
Introduced FtPartsDesc and integrated FtPartsVis into Fighter/ftData layout.
Added TempS, FtPartsVisLookup, and FtPartsVis structures used by matched ftparts model visibility code.
Renamed ftparts symbols such as ftPartsSetupRigidMtx, ftPartsRemap, and ftPartSetRotX/Y/Z.
Large call-site update for Kirby hat parts, replacing raw count fields with DObjList/FtPartsVis/FtPartsDesc usage.
Updated Purin-specific ftParts_8007487C usage to the new FtPartsDesc/FtPartsVis/DObjList signature.

what_changed: Linked ftparts by switching Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftparts.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftparts.c") in configure.py.
Implemented ftParts_8007487C with a typed signature: FtPartsDesc* desc, FtPartsVis* vis, u32 costume_id, DObjList* arg3, DObjList* arg4.

ftParts_8007487C now copies desc->model_num into vis->model_num, asserts when the model count is over 11 with "fighter parts model num over!", and chooses costume-specific visibility table entries with fallback to costume 0 entries.
Implemented/modelled ftParts_80074B6C, ftParts_80074CA0, and ftParts_80074D7C around FtPartsVisLookup, vis->cleared, vis->model_num, and DObjList rather than raw u32/count pointers.
Added FtPartsVis, FtPartsVisLookup, and TempS in dobjlist.h, and added FtPartsDesc in types.h.
Changed Fighter layout around fp+5AC from loose x5AC/x5C8-style fields to FtPartsVis x5AC, with call sites using x5AC.model_num and x5AC.xC[n].
Renamed model-index bookkeeping fields from x5F4_arr[i].x0/x1 to x5F4_arr[i].prev/idx and updated related users.

Renamed several address-based ftparts functions in symbols and headers, including ftParts_80073830 to ftPartsSetupRigidMtx, ftParts_800739B8 to ftPartsSetupSharedVtxMtx, ftParts_80073CA8 to ftPartsSetupEnvelopeMtx, ftParts_80074148 to ftPartsPObjSetDefaultClass, ftParts_80074170 to ftPartsPObjClearDefaultClass, ftParts_80075028 to ftPartsRemap, ftParts_8007592C to ftPartSetRotX, ftParts_80075AF0 to ftPartSetRotY, ftParts_80075CB4 to ftPartSetRotZ, ftParts_80075E78 to ftPartGetRotX, and ftParts_80075F48 to ftPartGetRotZ.

Updated many fighter modules to call the renamed rotation APIs and new ftParts_8007487C signature, including Captain, Common, Fox, GameWatch, Kirby, Koopa, Ness, Pikachu, Popo/Nana, Purin, Samus, Yoshi, and Zelda files.
Adjusted KirbyHatStruct and Kirby/Purin fighter-specific data so hat/item part descriptors use FtPartsDesc and FtPartsVis instead of raw joint_num/hat_vis_table/u32 placeholders.
smart_moves: Converted ambiguous raw fighter fields into small domain-specific structs before or while matching the target functions; this made ftparts model-visibility logic expressible without relying on pointer arithmetic.
Renamed address-based helpers based on observed behavior at call sites, especially rotation setters/getters and PObj default-class setup/cleanup, making widespread replacements self-documenting.
Preserved matching-sensitive details in ftParts_8007487C, including the model-count assert string and costume visibility fallback expression.
Reused one coherent FtPartsDesc shape for base fighter data, Kirby hats, and Purin/item-like special parts instead of treating each call site as an unrelated cast.
Updated symbols.txt in lockstep with function renames so linked symbols remained searchable and meaningful after the match.
decomp_lessons: When a function takes several u32* or UNK_T arguments but call sites repeatedly pass DObjList.count or adjacent fields, consider that the real argument may be a containing struct such as DObjList or FtPartsVis rather than the scalar subfield.
Matching a central fighter utility often requires cleaning up many character-specific call sites; the call-site churn can be evidence for field names and prototypes.

For model visibility tables, represent the descriptor separately from the runtime visibility state: FtPartsDesc holds model_num and vis_table, while FtPartsVis holds model_num, cleared flags, and resolved lookup pointers.

Address-based rotation functions can often be named from the axis and operation visible in their arguments and consumers: SetRotX/Y/Z and GetRotX/Z were supported by many call sites using atan2f, M_PI_2, and blend-frame reads.
Do not leave data-section filler strings once the function using them is matched; ftParts_8007487C absorbed the previous filler assert string directly into the implementation.
Switching an object to Matching should happen only after headers, symbols, and dependent call sites agree on the typed ABI; this PR made ftparts.c linkable rather than only locally matched.
naming_conventions: Use ftParts* for broader fighter-parts subsystem routines, e.g. ftPartsSetupRigidMtx, ftPartsSetupSharedVtxMtx, ftPartsSetupEnvelopeMtx, ftPartsRemap.
Use singular ftPart* for operations on one fighter part/bone, e.g. ftPartSetRotX, ftPartSetRotY, ftPartSetRotZ, ftPartGetRotX, ftPartGetRotZ.
Use Set/Clear paired names for global or default-class toggles: ftPartsPObjSetDefaultClass and ftPartsPObjClearDefaultClass.
Prefer semantic fields prev and idx over x0/x1 for x5F4_arr entries when tracking previous/current model visibility indices.
Use FtPartsDesc for packed model-count plus visibility-table descriptors and FtPartsVis for runtime resolved visibility data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed function prototypes from raw pointers to containing structs to recover the expected code shape and argument access patterns.
Used explicit fallback ternaries for vis_table[costume_id][n] ? vis_table[costume_id][n] : vis_table[0][n] in ftParts_8007487C.
Kept PAD_STACK(0x8) in ftParts_8007487C, indicating stack-shape sensitivity for matching.
Used x5AC.model_num loop bounds instead of raw fp->x5AC, aligning generated loads with the recovered FtPartsVis layout.
Replaced magic 0.01745329238474369f with deg_to_rad in at least one Fox reflector turn helper while preserving the same intended degrees-to-radians operation.
Used typed array access such as u8 (*blend_data)[2] = &fp->x28[anim_id] in ftwaitanim to clarify two-byte blend data and support matching.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code +1084 bytes and linked code +10844 bytes.
review_feedback.details: New matches listed for main/melee/ft/ftparts: ftParts_8007487C (+336), ftParts_80074B6C (+308), ftParts_80074CA0 (+220), ftParts_80074D7C (+220), and .sdata (+3).

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
review_feedback.details: The PR body was null and review_comments_excerpt was empty, so reviewer intent beyond merge acceptance is not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining ftParts_80074xxx functions after PR 2069.
Search for remaining uses of x5AC.xC or FtPartsVisLookup to refine TempS/FtPartsVisLookup field names.
Check whether ftPartGetRotY exists or whether only X/Z getters are required by the game code.
Look for follow-up PRs that further type ftParts_80075650, ftParts_80075240, or ftParts_80075304.
Search for additional DObjList.count-to-DObjList pointer corrections in fighter special accessory code.
